1→# .emacs.d
     2→
     3→Modern Emacs configuration optimized for Emacs 30+ using best practices and contemporary tools.
     4→
     5→## Overview
     6→
     7→This configuration has been fully modernized (January 2026) to use:
     8→- **use-package** for declarative package management
     9→- **Vertico ecosystem** for lightweight, fast completion
    10→- **Eglot** for LSP-powered code intelligence
    11→- **Tree-sitter** for accurate syntax highlighting (with fallbacks)
    12→- **early-init.el** for zero-flash startup
    13→- **Pixel-precise scrolling** for native macOS trackpad feel
    14→- **no-littering** for clean repository structure
    15→- **Modern advice-add** (no deprecated code)
    16→- **Single init.el** (consolidated from 6 files)
    17→
    18→## Quick Start
    19→
    20→```bash
    21→# Clone the repository
    22→git clone https://github.com/nsega/.emacs.d.git ~/.emacs.d
    23→
    24→# Install dependencies (see below)
    25→brew install cmigemo ripgrep
    26→
    27→# Launch Emacs (packages install automatically)
    28→emacs
    29→```
    30→
    31→For detailed usage, see the **[Learning Resources](#learning-resources)** below.
    32→
    33→## Requirements
    34→
    35→- **Emacs 30.x** or later (29.x also works)
    36→- **macOS** (tested on Apple Silicon)
    37→- **Homebrew** for package management
    38→
    39→### Optional but Recommended
    40→```bash
    41→brew install ripgrep      # Fast project search
    42→brew install pandoc       # Markdown preview
    43→```
    44→
    45→## Key Features
    46→
    47→### Completion Framework (Vertico Ecosystem)
    48→- **vertico** - Fast vertical completion UI
    49→- **consult** - Enhanced commands (buffer switching, search, etc.)
    50→- **marginalia** - Rich annotations in minibuffer
    51→- **embark** - Contextual actions on completion candidates
    52→- **orderless** - Flexible matching (type words in any order!)
    53→
    54→### Code Intelligence (Eglot LSP)
    55→- Jump to definition / Find references
    56→- Code completion with Company
    57→- Real-time diagnostics
    58→- Symbol renaming across files
    59→- Documentation on hover
    60→
    61→### Project Management
    62→- **project.el** (built-in) - Native project-aware navigation
    63→- Auto-detects project roots (git, go.mod, etc.)
    64→- Fast file finding and project-wide search
    65→- Standard `C-x p` prefix (Emacs default)
    66→
    67→### Editing Enhancements
    68→- **yasnippet** - Template system
    69→- **smartparens** - Smart parenthesis handling
    70→- **iedit** - Multi-cursor editing
    71→- **undo-tree** - Visual undo history
    72→- Smart line copy/cut (no selection needed)
    73→- Auto-indent on yank
    74→
    75→### macOS Optimizations
    76→- **Pixel-scroll-precision-mode** - Smooth trackpad scrolling (feels native)
    77→- **early-init.el** - Zero-flash startup (UI disabled before frame init)
    78→- **exec-path-from-shell** - Proper PATH inheritance from shell
    79→- **no-littering** - Clean .emacs.d (files organized in var/ and etc/)
    80→
    81→### Language Support
    82→| Language   | Mode               | LSP Server                     | Install Command |
    83→|------------|--------------------|--------------------------------|-----------------|
    84→| Python     | python-ts-mode     | pylsp / basedpyright           | `pip install python-lsp-server` |
    85→| Go         | go-ts-mode         | gopls                          | `go install golang.org/x/tools/gopls@latest` |
    86→| JavaScript | js-ts-mode         | typescript-language-server     | `npm install -g typescript-language-server typescript` |
    87→| TypeScript | typescript-ts-mode | typescript-language-server     | `npm install -g typescript-language-server typescript` |
    88→| Java       | java-ts-mode       | jdtls                          | `brew install jdtls` |
    89→| Kotlin     | kotlin-mode        | kotlin-language-server         | `brew install kotlin-language-server` |
    90→| Groovy     | groovy-mode        | -                              | (syntax highlighting only) |
    91→| C/C++      | c-ts-mode / c++-ts-mode | clangd                    | `xcode-select --install` or `brew install llvm` |
    92→| YAML       | yaml-ts-mode       | yaml-language-server (opt)     | `npm install -g yaml-language-server` |
    93→| Markdown   | markdown-mode      | -                              | `brew install pandoc` (for preview) |
    94→
    95→**Tree-sitter modes** (`*-ts-mode`) provide accurate syntax highlighting. Grammars install via `M-x treesit-install-language-grammar`.
    96→
    97→### Terminal Integration
    98→- **vterm** - Full-featured terminal emulator
    99→- **eat** - Pure elisp terminal (fallback)
   100→- **Claude Code** - AI pair programming with Claude CLI
   101→
   102→#### Claude Code Key Bindings
   103→| Key | Command | Description |
   104→|-----|---------|-------------|
   105→| `C-c c c` | claude-code | Start Claude Code session |
   106→| `C-c c r` | Send region | Send selected code to Claude |
   107→| `C-c c s` | Send command | Send a command to Claude |
   108→| `C-c c t` | Toggle window | Show/hide Claude window |
   109→| `C-c c k` | Kill instance | Kill Claude session |
   110→
   111→### Markdown Preview
   112→Live preview with side-by-side editing using EWW (built-in browser).
   113→
   114→| Key | Command | Description |
   115→|-----|---------|-------------|
   116→| `C-c C-c l` | Live preview | Toggle live preview (updates as you type) |
   117→| `C-c C-c p` | Preview | Open preview in external browser |
   118→| `C-c C-c v` | Export & view | Export HTML and open in browser |
   119→| `C-c C-c g` | grip-mode | GitHub-flavored preview (exact GitHub rendering) |
   120→
   121→**Mermaid diagram support:** Install `npm install -g @mermaid-js/mermaid-cli mermaid-filter` to render Mermaid diagrams. Use `C-c C-c p` (external browser) for Mermaid since EWW has limited image support.
   122→
   123→**GitHub preview:** Run `go install github.com/chrishrb/go-grip@latest` for GitHub-flavored rendering with `C-c C-c g` (works offline, no API limits).
   124→
   125→## Installation
   126→
   127→### 1. Clone Repository
   128→```bash
   129→git clone https://github.com/nsega/.emacs.d.git ~/.emacs.d
   130→```
   131→
   132→### 2. Install System Dependencies
   133→```bash
   134→# Required for Japanese search
   135→brew install cmigemo
   136→
   137→# Highly recommended for fast search
   138→brew install ripgrep
   139→
   140→# Optional: Markdown support
   141→brew install pandoc
   142→
   143→# Terminal support (required for Claude Code)
   144→brew install libvterm
   145→
   146→# Claude Code CLI (for AI pair programming)
   147→brew install --cask claude-code
   148→```
   149→
   150→### 3. Install LSP Servers (for code intelligence)
   151→
   152→#### Python
   153→```bash
   154→pip install python-lsp-server
   155→# Or for better performance:
   156→pip install basedpyright
   157→```
   158→
   159→#### Go
   160→```bash
   161→go install golang.org/x/tools/gopls@latest
   162→```
   163→
   164→#### TypeScript
   165→```bash
   166→npm install -g typescript-language-server typescript
   167→```
   168→
   169→#### Java
   170→```bash
   171→brew install jdtls
   172→```
   173→
   174→#### Kotlin
   175→```bash
   176→brew install kotlin-language-server
   177→```
   178→
   179→#### YAML (optional)
   180→```bash
   181→npm install -g yaml-language-server
   182→```
   183→
   184→### 4. Install Tree-sitter Grammars (optional but recommended)
   185→
   186→Tree-sitter provides accurate syntax highlighting. Install grammars in Emacs:
   187→```
   188→M-x treesit-install-language-grammar RET c
   189→M-x treesit-install-language-grammar RET cpp
   190→M-x treesit-install-language-grammar RET python
   191→M-x treesit-install-language-grammar RET go
   192→M-x treesit-install-language-grammar RET javascript
   193→M-x treesit-install-language-grammar RET typescript
   194→M-x treesit-install-language-grammar RET java
   195→M-x treesit-install-language-grammar RET kotlin
   196→```
   197→
   198→### 5. Launch Emacs
   199→```bash
   200→emacs
   201→```
   202→
   203→On first launch, all packages install automatically from MELPA. This may take 1-2 minutes.
   204→
   205→## Essential Key Bindings
   206→
   207→### Most Important (Learn These First!)
   208→| Key | Command | Description |
   209→|-----|---------|-------------|
   210→| `M-.` | Jump to definition | LSP-powered navigation |
   211→| `M-,` | Go back | Like browser back button |
   212→| `M-?` | Find references | Show all uses of symbol |
   213→| `M-g i` | Jump in file | Jump to function/section (imenu) |
   214→| `M-s l` | Search buffer | Search with live preview |
   215→| `C-x p f` | Find file | Find file in project |
   216→| `C-x b` | Switch buffer | Buffer list with preview |
   217→
   218→### Completion (Vertico)
   219→| Key | Command | Description |
   220→|-----|---------|-------------|
   221→| `M-x` | Execute command | Command palette with vertico |
   222→| `C-x b` | consult-buffer | Switch buffers (shows recent files) |
   223→| `C-x C-f` | find-file | Find/open file |
   224→| `C-j / C-n` | Next item | In vertico completion |
   225→| `C-k / C-p` | Previous item | In vertico completion |
   226→
   227→### Search & Navigation
   228→| Key | Command | Description |
   229→|-----|---------|-------------|
   230→| `M-s l` | consult-line | Search lines in buffer (live!) |
   231→| `M-s r` | consult-ripgrep | Fast project-wide search |
   232→| `M-s g` | consult-grep | Grep search in directory |
   233→| `M-g i` | consult-imenu | Jump to function/section |
   234→
   235→### Project Management (project.el)
   236→| Key | Command | Description |
   237→|-----|---------|-------------|
   238→| `C-x p p` | Switch project | List known projects |
   239→| `C-x p f` | Find file | Find file in project |
   240→| `C-x p g` | Find regexp | Search across project |
   241→| `C-x p b` | Switch buffer | Project buffers only |
   242→| `C-x p k` | Kill buffers | Kill all project buffers |
   243→| `C-x p e` | Eshell | Open eshell in project root |
   244→
   245→### Code Intelligence (LSP via Eglot)
   246→| Key | Command | Description |
   247→|-----|---------|-------------|
   248→| `M-.` | xref-find-definitions | Jump to definition |
   249→| `M-?` | xref-find-references | Find all references |
   250→| `M-,` | xref-pop-marker-stack | Go back |
   251→| `C-h .` | eldoc-doc-buffer | Show documentation |
   252→| `C-c r` | eglot-rename | Rename symbol everywhere |
   253→
   254→### Editing
   255→| Key | Command | Description |
   256→|-----|---------|-------------|
   257→| `C-;` | iedit-mode | Edit all occurrences |
   258→| `M-%` | anzu-query-replace | Replace with count |
   259→| `C-M-%` | anzu-query-replace-regexp | Regex replace |
   260→| `M-w` | Copy line | Copy line (no selection needed) |
   261→| `C-w` | Cut line | Cut line (no selection needed) |
   262→| `C-c i` | Indent buffer/region | Auto-indent |
   263→| `C-a` | Smart beginning of line | Toggle indent/start |
   264→| `M-o` | Smart open line | Insert line below/above |
   265→
   266→### Advanced
   267→| Key | Command | Description |
   268→|-----|---------|-------------|
   269→| `C-.` | embark-act | Show actions for thing at point |
   270→| `C-h B` | embark-bindings | Show all key bindings |
   271→
   272→For complete reference, see **[USER_GUIDE.md](USER_GUIDE.md)**
   273→
   274→## Learning Resources
   275→
   276→This repository includes comprehensive documentation:
   277→
   278→### 1. **[USER_GUIDE.md](USER_GUIDE.md)** - Complete Reference
   279→The comprehensive guide covering everything you need:
   280→- Quick reference: Essential key bindings organized by category
   281→- Workflows: 6 common patterns for reading code
   282→- Terminal tips: Terminal-specific compatibility and configuration
   283→- Advanced features: Power user techniques
   284→- Troubleshooting: Solutions to common problems
   285→
   286→### 2. **[TUTORIAL.md](TUTORIAL.md)** - Hands-On Exercises
   287→Step-by-step interactive tutorial:
   288→- 6 exercises using real demo code (Go projects)
   289→- Obsidian vault navigation examples
   290→- Power user workflows
   291→- Practice challenges
   292→- Real-world tasks
   293→
   294→**Recommended learning path:**
   295→1. Read USER_GUIDE.md Quick Reference section
   296→2. Try exercises 1-3 in TUTORIAL.md
   297→3. Reference USER_GUIDE.md workflows as needed
   298→4. **Terminal users:** See USER_GUIDE.md Terminal Emacs section
   299→
   300→## Directory Structure
   301→
   302→```
   303→~/.emacs.d/
   304→├── init.el                      # Main configuration (single file!)
   305→├── early-init.el                # Early initialization (UI, GC optimization)
   306→├── README.md                    # This file
   307→├── USER_GUIDE.md               # Complete user guide (reference + workflows + terminal tips)
   308→├── TUTORIAL.md                 # Hands-on exercises and tutorials
   309→├── CLAUDE.md                   # Guidelines for Claude Code
   310→├── LICENSE                     # MIT License
   311→├── .claude/
   312→│   ├── plans/
   313→│   │   └── modernization-for-emacs30.md  # Modernization plan
   314→│   └── settings.local.json     # Local Claude settings
   315→├── elpa/                       # Installed packages (auto-generated)
   316→├── snippets/                   # Yasnippet templates
   317→├── tree-sitter/                # Tree-sitter grammars (auto-generated)
   318→└── custom.backup.*/            # Backups from modernization
   319→```
   320→
   321→**Note:** The old `custom/` directory structure has been consolidated into a single `init.el` using use-package.
   322→
   323→## Usage Examples
   324→
   325→### Opening a Project
   326→```bash
   327→cd /path/to/your/project
   328→emacs .
   329→```
   330→
   331→Emacs opens in Dired (directory editor):
   332→- Press `RET` on files to open them
   333→- Press `^` to go up to parent directory
   334→- Or use `C-x p f` to find files quickly
   335→
   336→### Exploring Code
   337→```
   338→1. C-x p f     → Find file in project
   339→2. M-g i       → See all functions
   340→3. M-.         → Jump to definition
   341→4. M-?         → Find references
   342→5. M-,         → Go back
   343→```
   344→
   345→### Searching Across Project
   346→```
   347→M-s r          → Type search term
   348→→ Navigate results with C-n/C-p
   349→→ Press RET to jump to match
   350→```
   351→
   352→### Renaming a Symbol
   353→```
   354→M-.            → Jump to definition
   355→C-c r          → eglot-rename
   356→→ Type new name
   357→→ RET          → Renames everywhere!
   358→```
   359→
   360→## Customization
   361→
   362→### Adding New Packages
   363→
   364→All packages use `use-package`. Add to `init.el`:
   365→
   366→```elisp
   367→(use-package your-package
   368→  :config
   369→  ;; Your configuration
   370→  :bind (("C-c y" . your-command)))
   371→```
   372→
   373→### Configuring LSP for New Languages
   374→
   375→```elisp
   376→(use-package your-mode
   377→  :hook (your-mode . eglot-ensure)
   378→  :config
   379→  ;; Optional: custom LSP server
   380→  (add-to-list 'eglot-server-programs
   381→               '(your-mode . ("your-lsp-server" "--args"))))
   382→```
   383→
   384→### Performance Tuning
   385→
   386→Check package load times:
   387→```
   388→M-x use-package-report
   389→```
   390→
   391→## Troubleshooting
   392→
   393→### LSP Not Working?
   394→
   395→1. **Check if Eglot is active:** Look for "Eglot" in mode line
   396→2. **Manually start:** `M-x eglot-ensure`
   397→3. **Restart server:** `M-x eglot-reconnect`
   398→4. **Check server is installed:**
   399→   ```bash
   400→   which gopls
   401→   which pylsp
   402→   which typescript-language-server
   403→   ```
   404→
   405→### Search is Slow?
   406→
   407→Install ripgrep for blazing fast search:
   408→```bash
   409→brew install ripgrep
   410→```
   411→Then use `M-s r` instead of `M-s g`
   412→
   413→### Packages Won't Install?
   414→
   415→```bash
   416→# Clean package cache
   417→rm -rf ~/.emacs.d/elpa
   418→emacs  # Packages reinstall automatically
   419→```
   420→
   421→### Can't Find Commands?
   422→
   423→Check available commands:
   424→```
   425→M-x consult-  [TAB]    # See all consult commands
   426→M-x project-  [TAB]    # See all project commands
   427→C-h B                  # Show all key bindings
   428→```
   429→
   430→### Japanese Input Not Working?
   431→
   432→```bash
   433→brew install cmigemo
   434→```
   435→
   436→Verify dictionary path in init.el matches:
   437→```bash
   438→ls /opt/homebrew/share/migemo/utf-8/migemo-dict
   439→```
   440→
   441→### Emacs Opens in Home Directory Instead of Current Directory?
   442→
   443→This was fixed in the modernization. Make sure you have the latest version:
   444→```bash
   445→cd ~/.emacs.d
   446→git pull
   447→```
   448→
   449→## What's Different from Traditional Configs?
   450→
   451→### Before Modernization
   452→- 6 separate files (init.el + custom/*.el)
   453→- Helm for completion (heavy)
   454→- Multiple navigation systems (helm-gtags, ggtags, CEDET)
   455→- Deprecated `defadvice` code
   456→- Manual package management
   457→
   458→### After Modernization
   459→- **Single init.el** with use-package
   460→- **Vertico ecosystem** (20-30% faster)
   461→- **Single LSP system** (Eglot only)
   462→- **Tree-sitter parsing** (accurate syntax highlighting)
   463→- **early-init.el** (zero-flash startup)
   464→- **Pixel-precise scrolling** (native macOS feel)
   465→- **no-littering** (clean directory structure)
   466→- **Modern advice-add** (no deprecated code)
   467→- **Declarative package management**
   468→
   469→**Result:** Simpler, faster, more maintainable, and feels like a native macOS app!
   470→
   471→## Advanced Features
   472→
   473→### Orderless Matching
   474→
   475→Type search terms in any order:
   476→- `"user test"` matches `test_user_function` or `user_management_test`
   477→- Works in all vertico completions
   478→
   479→### Live Previews
   480→
   481→- `C-x b` - Preview buffers before switching
   482→- `M-s l` - See matches as you type
   483→- `C-c C-c l` - Markdown live preview (in markdown files)
   484→- Navigate with `C-n`/`C-p` to peek
   485→
   486→### Multi-Edit (iedit)
   487→
   488→```
   489→M-s l          → Find pattern
   490→C-;            → Edit all occurrences at once
   491→→ Type changes
   492→C-;            → Exit iedit
   493→```
   494→
   495→### Embark Actions
   496→
   497→```
   498→C-x b          → Buffer list
   499→→ Select buffer
   500→C-.            → Show actions (view, save, kill, diff, etc.)
   501→```
   502→
   503→## Performance
   504→
   505→- **Startup time:** ~2-3 seconds (packages lazy-load)
   506→- **Search:** < 0.1s for most projects (with ripgrep)
   507→- **LSP response:** Real-time (< 100ms)
   508→- **Memory:** ~50-100MB base, ~200MB with multiple LSP servers
   509→
   510→Use `M-x use-package-report` to analyze package load times.
   511→
   512→## Contributing
   513→
   514→This is a personal configuration, but improvements are welcome:
   515→
   516→1. Fork the repository
   517→2. Create a feature branch
   518→3. Make your changes
   519→4. Submit a pull request
   520→
   521→## License
   522→
   523→MIT License - See LICENSE file for details
   524→
   525→## Acknowledgments
   526→
   527→Built with these amazing packages:
   528→- [vertico](https://github.com/minad/vertico) - Vertical completion
   529→- [consult](https://github.com/minad/consult) - Consulting completing-read
   530→- [marginalia](https://github.com/minad/marginalia) - Rich annotations
   531→- [embark](https://github.com/oantolin/embark) - Contextual actions
   532→- [company](https://github.com/company-mode/company-mode) - Completion
   533→- [eglot](https://github.com/joaotavora/eglot) - LSP client
   534→- [claude-code.el](https://github.com/stevemolitor/claude-code.el) - Claude Code integration
   535→- [vterm](https://github.com/akermu/emacs-libvterm) - Terminal emulator
   536→
   537→## Additional Resources
   538→
   539→- [Emacs Wiki](https://www.emacswiki.org/)
   540→- [Vertico Documentation](https://github.com/minad/vertico)
   541→- [Eglot Manual](https://joaotavora.github.io/eglot/)
   542→- [project.el Manual](https://www.gnu.org/software/emacs/manual/html_node/emacs/Projects.html)
   543→
   544→---
   545→
   546→**Last Updated:** January 2026
   547→**Emacs Version:** 30.x
   548→**Status:** Production Ready
   549→
   550→For questions or issues, please open an issue on GitHub.
   551→

file.content:
# .emacs.d

Modern Emacs configuration optimized for Emacs 30+ using best practices and contemporary tools.

## Overview

This configuration has been fully modernized (January 2026) to use:
- **use-package** for declarative package management
- **Vertico ecosystem** for lightweight, fast completion
- **Eglot** for LSP-powered code intelligence
- **Tree-sitter** for accurate syntax highlighting (with fallbacks)
- **early-init.el** for zero-flash startup
- **Pixel-precise scrolling** for native macOS trackpad feel
- **no-littering** for clean repository structure
- **Modern advice-add** (no deprecated code)
- **Single init.el** (consolidated from 6 files)

## Quick Start

```bash
# Clone the repository
git clone https://github.com/nsega/.emacs.d.git ~/.emacs.d

# Install dependencies (see below)
brew install cmigemo ripgrep

# Launch Emacs (packages install automatically)
emacs
```

For detailed usage, see the **[Learning Resources](#learning-resources)** below.

## Requirements

- **Emacs 30.x** or later (29.x also works)
- **macOS** (tested on Apple Silicon)
- **Homebrew** for package management

### Optional but Recommended
```bash
brew install ripgrep      # Fast project search
brew install pandoc       # Markdown preview
```

## Key Features

### Completion Framework (Vertico Ecosystem)
- **vertico** - Fast vertical completion UI
- **consult** - Enhanced commands (buffer switching, search, etc.)
- **marginalia** - Rich annotations in minibuffer
- **embark** - Contextual actions on completion candidates
- **orderless** - Flexible matching (type words in any order!)

### Code Intelligence (Eglot LSP)
- Jump to definition / Find references
- Code completion with Company
- Real-time diagnostics
- Symbol renaming across files
- Documentation on hover

### Project Management
- **project.el** (built-in) - Native project-aware navigation
- Auto-detects project roots (git, go.mod, etc.)
- Fast file finding and project-wide search
- Standard `C-x p` prefix (Emacs default)

### Editing Enhancements
- **yasnippet** - Template system
- **smartparens** - Smart parenthesis handling
- **iedit** - Multi-cursor editing
- **undo-tree** - Visual undo history
- Smart line copy/cut (no selection needed)
- Auto-indent on yank

### macOS Optimizations
- **Pixel-scroll-precision-mode** - Smooth trackpad scrolling (feels native)
- **early-init.el** - Zero-flash startup (UI disabled before frame init)
- **exec-path-from-shell** - Proper PATH inheritance from shell
- **no-littering** - Clean .emacs.d (files organized in var/ and etc/)

### Language Support
| Language   | Mode               | LSP Server                     | Install Command |
|------------|--------------------|--------------------------------|-----------------|
| Python     | python-ts-mode     | pylsp / basedpyright           | `pip install python-lsp-server` |
| Go         | go-ts-mode         | gopls                          | `go install golang.org/x/tools/gopls@latest` |
| JavaScript | js-ts-mode         | typescript-language-server     | `npm install -g typescript-language-server typescript` |
| TypeScript | typescript-ts-mode | typescript-language-server     | `npm install -g typescript-language-server typescript` |
| Java       | java-ts-mode       | jdtls                          | `brew install jdtls` |
| Kotlin     | kotlin-mode        | kotlin-language-server         | `brew install kotlin-language-server` |
| Groovy     | groovy-mode        | -                              | (syntax highlighting only) |
| C/C++      | c-ts-mode / c++-ts-mode | clangd                    | `xcode-select --install` or `brew install llvm` |
| YAML       | yaml-ts-mode       | yaml-language-server (opt)     | `npm install -g yaml-language-server` |
| Markdown   | markdown-mode      | -                              | `brew install pandoc` (for preview) |

**Tree-sitter modes** (`*-ts-mode`) provide accurate syntax highlighting. Grammars install via `M-x treesit-install-language-grammar`.

### Terminal Integration
- **vterm** - Full-featured terminal emulator
- **eat** - Pure elisp terminal (fallback)
- **Claude Code** - AI pair programming with Claude CLI

#### Claude Code Key Bindings
| Key | Command | Description |
|-----|---------|-------------|
| `C-c c c` | claude-code | Start Claude Code session |
| `C-c c r` | Send region | Send selected code to Claude |
| `C-c c s` | Send command | Send a command to Claude |
| `C-c c t` | Toggle window | Show/hide Claude window |
| `C-c c k` | Kill instance | Kill Claude session |

### Markdown Preview
Live preview with side-by-side editing using EWW (built-in browser).

| Key | Command | Description |
|-----|---------|-------------|
| `C-c C-c l` | Live preview | Toggle live preview (updates as you type) |
| `C-c C-c p` | Preview | Open preview in external browser |
| `C-c C-c v` | Export & view | Export HTML and open in browser |
| `C-c C-c g` | grip-mode | GitHub-flavored preview (exact GitHub rendering) |

**Mermaid diagram support:** Install `npm install -g @mermaid-js/mermaid-cli mermaid-filter` to render Mermaid diagrams. Use `C-c C-c p` (external browser) for Mermaid since EWW has limited image support.

**GitHub preview:** Run `go install github.com/chrishrb/go-grip@latest` for GitHub-flavored rendering with `C-c C-c g` (works offline, no API limits).

## Installation

### 1. Clone Repository
```bash
git clone https://github.com/nsega/.emacs.d.git ~/.emacs.d
```

### 2. Install System Dependencies
```bash
# Required for Japanese search
brew install cmigemo

# Highly recommended for fast search
brew install ripgrep

# Optional: Markdown support
brew install pandoc

# Terminal support (required for Claude Code)
brew install libvterm

# Claude Code CLI (for AI pair programming)
brew install --cask claude-code
```

### 3. Install LSP Servers (for code intelligence)

#### Python
```bash
pip install python-lsp-server
# Or for better performance:
pip install basedpyright
```

#### Go
```bash
go install golang.org/x/tools/gopls@latest
```

#### TypeScript
```bash
npm install -g typescript-language-server typescript
```

#### Java
```bash
brew install jdtls
```

#### Kotlin
```bash
brew install kotlin-language-server
```

#### YAML (optional)
```bash
npm install -g yaml-language-server
```

### 4. Install Tree-sitter Grammars (optional but recommended)

Tree-sitter provides accurate syntax highlighting. Install grammars in Emacs:
```
M-x treesit-install-language-grammar RET c
M-x treesit-install-language-grammar RET cpp
M-x treesit-install-language-grammar RET python
M-x treesit-install-language-grammar RET go
M-x treesit-install-language-grammar RET javascript
M-x treesit-install-language-grammar RET typescript
M-x treesit-install-language-grammar RET java
M-x treesit-install-language-grammar RET kotlin
```

### 5. Launch Emacs
```bash
emacs
```

On first launch, all packages install automatically from MELPA. This may take 1-2 minutes.

## Essential Key Bindings

### Most Important (Learn These First!)
| Key | Command | Description |
|-----|---------|-------------|
| `M-.` | Jump to definition | LSP-powered navigation |
| `M-,` | Go back | Like browser back button |
| `M-?` | Find references | Show all uses of symbol |
| `M-g i` | Jump in file | Jump to function/section (imenu) |
| `M-s l` | Search buffer | Search with live preview |
| `C-x p f` | Find file | Find file in project |
| `C-x b` | Switch buffer | Buffer list with preview |

### Completion (Vertico)

|-----|---------|-------------|
| `M-x` | Execute command | Command palette with vertico |

| `C-j / C-n` | Next item | In vertico completion |
| `C-k / C-p` | Previous item | In vertico completion |

### Search & Navigation
| Key | Command | Description |
|-----|---------|-------------|
| `M-s l` | consult-line | Search lines in buffer (live!) |
| `M-s r` | consult-ripgrep | Fast project-wide search |

| `M-g i` | consult-imenu | Jump to function/section |

### Project Management (project.el)
| Key | Command | Description |
|-----|---------|-------------|
| `C-x p p` | Switch project | List known projects |
| `C-x p f` | Find file | Find file in project |

| `C-x p b` | Switch buffer | Project buffers only |

### Code Intelligence (LSP via Eglot)

### Editing

3. Reference USER_GUIDE.md workflows as needed

## Directory Structure

```
~/.emacs.d/
├── init.el                      # Main configuration (single file!)
├── early-init.el                # Early initialization (UI, GC optimization)
├── README.md                    # This file
├── USER_GUIDE.md               # Complete user guide (reference + workflows + terminal tips)
├── TUTORIAL.md                 # Hands-on exercises and tutorials
├── CLAUDE.md                   # Guidelines for Claude Code
├── LICENSE                     # MIT License
├── .claude/
│   ├── plans/
│   │   └── modernization-for-emacs30.md  # Modernization plan
│   └── settings.local.json     # Local Claude settings
├── elpa/                       # Installed packages (auto-generated)
├── snippets/                   # Yasnippet templates
├── tree-sitter/                # Tree-sitter grammars (auto-generated)
└── custom.backup.*/            # Backups from modernization
```

**Note:** The old `custom/` directory structure has been consolidated into a single `init.el` using use-package.

## Usage Examples

### Opening a Project
```bash
cd /path/to/your/project
emacs .
```

Emacs opens in Dired (directory editor):
- Press `RET` on files to open them
- Press `^` to go up to parent directory
- Or use `C-x p f` to find files quickly

### Exploring Code
```
1. C-x p f     → Find file in project
2. M-g i       → See all functions
3. M-.         → Jump to definition
4. M-?         → Find references
5. M-,         → Go back
```

### Searching Across Project
```
M-s r          → Type search term
→ Navigate results with C-n/C-p
→ Press RET to jump to match
```

### Renaming a Symbol
```
M-.            → Jump to definition
C-c r          → eglot-rename
→ Type new name
→ RET          → Renames everywhere!
```

## Customization

### Adding New Packages

All packages use `use-package`. Add to `init.el`:

```elisp
(use-package your-package
  :config
  ;; Your configuration
  :bind (("C-c y" . your-command)))
```

### Configuring LSP for New Languages

```elisp
(use-package your-mode
  :hook (your-mode . eglot-ensure)
  :config
  ;; Optional: custom LSP server
  (add-to-list 'eglot-server-programs
               '(your-mode . ("your-lsp-server" "--args"))))
```

### Performance Tuning

Check package load times:
```
M-x use-package-report
```

## Troubleshooting

### LSP Not Working?

1. **Check if Eglot is active:** Look for "Eglot" in mode line
2. **Manually start:** `M-x eglot-ensure`
3. **Restart server:** `M-x eglot-reconnect`
4. **Check server is installed:**
   ```bash
   which gopls
   which pylsp
   which typescript-language-server
   ```

### Search is Slow?

Install ripgrep for blazing fast search:
```bash
brew install ripgrep
```
Then use `M-s r` instead of `M-s g`

### Packages Won't Install?

```bash
# Clean package cache
rm -rf ~/.emacs.d/elpa
emacs  # Packages reinstall automatically
```

### Can't Find Commands?

Check available commands:
```
M-x consult-  [TAB]    # See all consult commands
M-x project-  [TAB]    # See all project commands
C-h B                  # Show all key bindings
```

### Japanese Input Not Working?

```bash
brew install cmigemo
```

Verify dictionary path in init.el matches:
```bash
ls /opt/homebrew/share/migemo/utf-8/migemo-dict
```

### Emacs Opens in Home Directory Instead of Current Directory?

This was fixed in the modernization. Make sure you have the latest version:
```bash
cd ~/.emacs.d
git pull
```

## What's Different from Traditional Configs?

### Before Modernization
- 6 separate files (init.el + custom/*.el)
- Helm for completion (heavy)
- Multiple navigation systems (helm-gtags, ggtags, CEDET)
- Deprecated `defadvice` code
- Manual package management

### After Modernization
- **Single init.el** with use-package
- **Vertico ecosystem** (20-30% faster)
- **Single LSP system** (Eglot only)
- **Tree-sitter parsing** (accurate syntax highlighting)
- **early-init.el** (zero-flash startup)
- **Pixel-precise scrolling** (native macOS feel)
- **no-littering** (clean directory structure)
- **Modern advice-add** (no deprecated code)
- **Declarative package management**

**Result:** Simpler, faster, more maintainable, and feels like a native macOS app!

## Advanced Features

### Orderless Matching

Type search terms in any order:
- `"user test"` matches `test_user_function` or `user_management_test`
- Works in all vertico completions

### Live Previews

- `C-x b` - Preview buffers before switching
- `M-s l` - See matches as you type
- `C-c C-c l` - Markdown live preview (in markdown files)
- Navigate with `C-n`/`C-p` to peek

### Multi-Edit (iedit)

```
M-s l          → Find pattern
C-;            → Edit all occurrences at once
→ Type changes
C-;            → Exit iedit
```

### Embark Actions

```
C-x b          → Buffer list
→ Select buffer
C-.            → Show actions (view, save, kill, diff, etc.)
```

## Performance

- **Startup time:** ~2-3 seconds (packages lazy-load)
- **Search:** < 0.1s for most projects (with ripgrep)
- **LSP response:** Real-time (< 100ms)
- **Memory:** ~50-100MB base, ~200MB with multiple LSP servers

Use `M-x use-package-report` to analyze package load times.

## Contributing

This is a personal configuration, but improvements are welcome:

1. Fork the repository
2. Create a feature branch
3. Make your changes
4. Submit a pull request

## Additional Resources

# .emacs.d

Modern Emacs configuration optimized for Emacs 30+ using best practices and contemporary tools.

## Overview

This configuration has been fully modernized (January 2026) to use:
- **use-package** for declarative package management
- **Vertico ecosystem** for lightweight, fast completion
- **Eglot** for LSP-powered code intelligence
- **Tree-sitter** for accurate syntax highlighting (with fallbacks)
- **early-init.el** for zero-flash startup
- **Pixel-precise scrolling** for native macOS trackpad feel
- **no-littering** for clean repository structure
- **Modern advice-add** (no deprecated code)
- **Single init.el** (consolidated from 6 files)

## Quick Start

```bash
# Clone the repository
git clone https://github.com/nsega/.emacs.d.git ~/.emacs.d

# Install dependencies (see below)
brew install cmigemo ripgrep

# Launch Emacs (packages install automatically)
emacs
```

For detailed usage, see the **[Learning Resources](#learning-resources)** below.

## Requirements

- **Emacs 30.x** or later (29.x also works)
- **macOS** (tested on Apple Silicon)
- **Homebrew** for package management

### Optional but Recommended
```bash
brew install ripgrep      # Fast project search
brew install pandoc       # Markdown preview
```

## Key Features

### Completion Framework (Vertico Ecosystem)
- **vertico** - Fast vertical completion UI
- **consult** - Enhanced commands (buffer switching, search, etc.)
- **marginalia** - Rich annotations in minibuffer
- **embark** - Contextual actions on completion candidates
- **orderless** - Flexible matching (type words in any order!)

### Code Intelligence (Eglot LSP)
- Jump to definition / Find references
- Code completion with Company
- Real-time diagnostics
- Symbol renaming across files
- Documentation on hover

### Project Management
- **project.el** (built-in) - Native project-aware navigation
- Auto-detects project roots (git, go.mod, etc.)
- Fast file finding and project-wide search
- Standard `C-x p` prefix (Emacs default)

### Editing Enhancements
- **yasnippet** - Template system
- **smartparens** - Smart parenthesis handling
- **iedit** - Multi-cursor editing
- **undo-tree** - Visual undo history
- Smart line copy/cut (no selection needed)
- Auto-indent on yank

### macOS Optimizations
- **Pixel-scroll-precision-mode** - Smooth trackpad scrolling (feels native)
- **early-init.el** - Zero-flash startup (UI disabled before frame init)
- **exec-path-from-shell** - Proper PATH inheritance from shell
- **no-littering** - Clean .emacs.d (files organized in var/ and etc/)

### Language Support
| Language   | Mode               | LSP Server                     | Install Command |
|------------|--------------------|--------------------------------|-----------------|
| Python     | python-ts-mode     | pylsp / basedpyright           | `pip install python-lsp-server` |
| Go         | go-ts-mode         | gopls                          | `go install golang.org/x/tools/gopls@latest` |
| JavaScript | js-ts-mode         | typescript-language-server     | `npm install -g typescript-language-server typescript` |
| TypeScript | typescript-ts-mode | typescript-language-server     | `npm install -g typescript-language-server typescript` |
| Java       | java-ts-mode       | jdtls                          | `brew install jdtls` |
| Kotlin     | kotlin-mode        | kotlin-language-server         | `brew install kotlin-language-server` |
| Groovy     | groovy-mode        | -                              | (syntax highlighting only) |
| C/C++      | c-ts-mode / c++-ts-mode | clangd                    | `xcode-select --install` or `brew install llvm` |
| YAML       | yaml-ts-mode       | yaml-language-server (opt)     | `npm install -g yaml-language-server` |
| Markdown   | markdown-mode      | -                              | `brew install pandoc` (for preview) |

**Tree-sitter modes** (`*-ts-mode`) provide accurate syntax highlighting. Grammars install via `M-x treesit-install-language-grammar`.

### Terminal Integration
- **vterm** - Full-featured terminal emulator
- **eat** - Pure elisp terminal (fallback)
- **Claude Code** - AI pair programming with Claude CLI

#### Claude Code Key Bindings
| Key | Command | Description |
|-----|---------|-------------|
| `C-c c c` | claude-code | Start Claude Code session |
| `C-c c r` | Send region | Send selected code to Claude |
| `C-c c s` | Send command | Send a command to Claude |
| `C-c c t` | Toggle window | Show/hide Claude window |
| `C-c c k` | Kill instance | Kill Claude session |

### Markdown Preview
Live preview with side-by-side editing using EWW (built-in browser).

| Key | Command | Description |
|-----|---------|-------------|
| `C-c C-c l` | Live preview | Toggle live preview (updates as you type) |
| `C-c C-c p` | Preview | Open preview in external browser |
| `C-c C-c v` | Export & view | Export HTML and open in browser |
| `C-c C-c g` | grip-mode | GitHub-flavored preview (exact GitHub rendering) |

**Mermaid diagram support:** Install `npm install -g @mermaid-js/mermaid-cli mermaid-filter` to render Mermaid diagrams. Use `C-c C-c p` (external browser) for Mermaid since EWW has limited image support.

**GitHub preview:** Run `go install github.com/chrishrb/go-grip@latest` for GitHub-flavored rendering with `C-c C-c g` (works offline, no API limits).




### 4. Install Tree-sitter Grammars (optional but recommended)

Tree-sitter provides accurate syntax highlighting. Install grammars in Emacs:
```

M-x treesit-install-language-grammar RET javascript
```

### 5. Launch Emacs

```bash
emacs
```

On first launch, all packages install automatically from MELPA. This may take 1-2 minutes.

```
├── early-init.el                # Early initialization (UI, GC optimization)
├── README.md                    # This file
├── USER_GUIDE.md               # Complete user guide (reference + workflows + terminal tips)
├── TUTORIAL.md                 # Hands-on exercises and tutorials
├── CLAUDE.md                   # Guidelines for Claude Code
├── LICENSE                     # MIT License
├── .claude/
│   ├── plans/
│   │   └── modernization-for-emacs30.md  # Modernization plan
│   └── settings.local.json     # Local Claude settings
├── elpa/                       # Installed packages (auto-generated)
├── snippets/                   # Yasnippet templates
├── tree-sitter/                # Tree-sitter grammars (auto-generated)
└── custom.backup.*/            # Backups from modernization
```

**Note:** The old `custom/` directory structure has been consolidated into a single `init.el` using use-package.

## Usage Examples

### Opening a Project
```bash
cd /path/to/your/project
emacs .
```

Emacs opens in Dired (directory editor):
- Press `RET` on files to open them
- Press `^` to go up to parent directory
- Or use `C-x p f` to find files quickly

### Exploring Code
```
1. C-x p f     → Find file in project
2. M-g i       → See all functions
3. M-.         → Jump to definition
4. M-?         → Find references
5. M-,         → Go back
```

### Searching Across Project
```
M-s r          → Type search term
→ Navigate results with C-n/C-p
→ Press RET to jump to match
```

### Renaming a Symbol
```
M-.            → Jump to definition
C-c r          → eglot-rename
→ Type new name
→ RET          → Renames everywhere!
```

## Customization

### Adding New Packages

All packages use `use-package`. Add to `init.el`:

```elisp
(use-package your-package
  :config
  ;; Your configuration
  :bind (("C-c y" . your-command)))
```

### Configuring LSP for New Languages

```elisp
(use-package your-mode
  :hook (your-mode . eglot-ensure)
  :config
  ;; Optional: custom LSP server
  (add-to-list 'eglot-server-programs
               '(your-mode . ("your-lsp-server" "--args"))))
```

### Performance Tuning

Check package load times:
```
M-x use-package-report
```

## Troubleshooting

### LSP Not Working?

1. **Check if Eglot is active:** Look for "Eglot" in mode line
2. **Manually start:** `M-x eglot-ensure`
3. **Restart server:** `M-x eglot-reconnect`
4. **Check server is installed:**
   ```bash
   which gopls
   which pylsp
   which typescript-language-server
   ```

### Search is Slow?

Install ripgrep for blazing fast search:
```bash
brew install ripgrep
```
Then use `M-s r` instead of `M-s g`

### Packages Won't Install?

```bash
# Clean package cache
rm -rf ~/.emacs.d/elpa
emacs  # Packages reinstall automatically
```

### Can't Find Commands?

Check available commands:
```
M-x consult-  [TAB]    # See all consult commands
M-x project-  [TAB]    # See all project commands
C-h B                  # Show all key bindings
```

### Japanese Input Not Working?

```bash
brew install cmigemo
```

Verify dictionary path in init.el matches:
```bash
ls /opt/homebrew/share/migemo/utf-8/migemo-dict
```

### Emacs Opens in Home Directory Instead of Current Directory?

This was fixed in the modernization. Make sure you have the latest version:
```bash
cd ~/.emacs.d
git pull
```

## What's Different from Traditional Configs?

### Before Modernization
- 6 separate files (init.el + custom/*.el)
- Helm for completion (heavy)
- Multiple navigation systems (helm-gtags, ggtags, CEDET)
- Deprecated `defadvice` code
- Manual package management

### After Modernization
- **Single init.el** with use-package
- **Vertico ecosystem** (20-30% faster)
- **Single LSP system** (Eglot only)
- **Tree-sitter parsing** (accurate syntax highlighting)
- **early-init.el** (zero-flash startup)
- **Pixel-precise scrolling** (native macOS feel)
- **no-littering** (clean directory structure)
- **Modern advice-add** (no deprecated code)
- **Declarative package management**

**Result:** Simpler, faster, more maintainable, and feels like a native macOS app!

## Advanced Features

### Orderless Matching

Type search terms in any order:
- `"user test"` matches `test_user_function` or `user_management_test`
- Works in all vertico completions

### Live Previews

- `C-x b` - Preview buffers before switching
- `M-s l` - See matches as you type
- `C-c C-c l` - Markdown live preview (in markdown files)
- Navigate with `C-n`/`C-p` to peek

### Multi-Edit (iedit)

```
M-s l          → Find pattern
C-;            → Edit all occurrences at once
→ Type changes
C-;            → Exit iedit
```

### Embark Actions

```
C-x b          → Buffer list
→ Select buffer
C-.            → Show actions (view, save, kill, diff, etc.)
```

## Performance

- **Startup time:** ~2-3 seconds (packages lazy-load)
- **Search:** < 0.1s for most projects (with ripgrep)
- **LSP response:** Real-time (< 100ms)
- **Memory:** ~50-100MB base, ~200MB with multiple LSP servers

Use `M-x use-package-report` to analyze package load times.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# .emacs.d

Modern Emacs configuration optimized for Emacs 30+ using best practices and contemporary tools.

## Overview

This configuration has been fully modernized (January 2026) to use:
- **use-package** for declarative package management
- **Vertico ecosystem** for lightweight, fast completion
- **Eglot** for LSP-powered code intelligence
- **Tree-sitter** for accurate syntax highlighting (with fallbacks)
- **early-init.el** for zero-flash startup
- **Pixel-precise scrolling** for native macOS trackpad feel
- **no-littering** for clean repository structure
- **Modern advice-add** (no deprecated code)
- **Single init.el** (consolidated from 6 files)

## Quick Start

```bash
# Clone the repository
git clone https://github.com/nsega/.emacs.d.git ~/.emacs.d

# Install dependencies (see below)
brew install cmigemo ripgrep

# Launch Emacs (packages install automatically)
emacs
```

For detailed usage, see the **[Learning Resources](#learning-resources)** below.

## Requirements

- **Emacs 30.x** or later (29.x also works)
- **macOS** (tested on Apple Silicon)
- **Homebrew** for package management

### Optional but Recommended
```bash
brew install ripgrep      # Fast project search
brew install pandoc       # Markdown preview
```

## Key Features

### Completion Framework (Vertico Ecosystem)
- **vertico** - Fast vertical completion UI
- **consult** - Enhanced commands (buffer switching, search, etc.)
- **marginalia** - Rich annotations in minibuffer
- **embark** - Contextual actions on completion candidates
- **orderless** - Flexible matching (type words in any order!)

### Code Intelligence (Eglot LSP)
- Jump to definition / Find references
- Code completion with Company
- Real-time diagnostics
- Symbol renaming across files
- Documentation on hover

### Project Management
- **project.el** (built-in) - Native project-aware navigation
- Auto-detects project roots (git, go.mod, etc.)
- Fast file finding and project-wide search
- Standard `C-x p` prefix (Emacs default)

### Editing Enhancements
- **yasnippet** - Template system
- **smartparens** - Smart parenthesis handling
- **iedit** - Multi-cursor editing
- **undo-tree** - Visual undo history
- Smart line copy/cut (no selection needed)
- Auto-indent on yank

### macOS Optimizations
- **Pixel-scroll-precision-mode** - Smooth trackpad scrolling (feels native)
- **early-init.el** - Zero-flash startup (UI disabled before frame init)
- **exec-path-from-shell** - Proper PATH inheritance from shell
- **no-littering** - Clean .emacs.d (files organized in var/ and etc/)

### Language Support
| Language   | Mode               | LSP Server                     | Install Command |
|------------|--------------------|--------------------------------|-----------------|
| Python     | python-ts-mode     | pylsp / basedpyright           | `pip install python-lsp-server` |
| Go         | go-ts-mode         | gopls                          | `go install golang.org/x/tools/gopls@latest` |
| JavaScript | js-ts-mode         | typescript-language-server     | `npm install -g typescript-language-server typescript` |
| TypeScript | typescript-ts-mode | typescript-language-server     | `npm install -g typescript-language-server typescript` |
| Java       | java-ts-mode       | jdtls                          | `brew install jdtls` |
| Kotlin     | kotlin-mode        | kotlin-language-server         | `brew install kotlin-language-server` |
| Groovy     | groovy-mode        | -                              | (syntax highlighting only) |
| C/C++      | c-ts-mode / c++-ts-mode | clangd                    | `xcode-select --install` or `brew install llvm` |
| YAML       | yaml-ts-mode       | yaml-language-server (opt)     | `npm install -g yaml-language-server` |

**Auto-formatting:** YAML files are automatically formatted on save using [apheleia](https://github.com/radian-software/apheleia) + [yamlfmt](https://github.com/google/yamlfmt). Install: `go install github.com/google/yamlfmt/cmd/yamlfmt@latest`
| Markdown   | markdown-mode      | -                              | `brew install pandoc` (for preview) |

**Tree-sitter modes** (`*-ts-mode`) provide accurate syntax highlighting. Grammars install via `M-x treesit-install-language-grammar`.

### Terminal Integration
- **vterm** - Full-featured terminal emulator
- **eat** - Pure elisp terminal (fallback)
- **Claude Code** - AI pair programming with Claude CLI

#### Claude Code Key Bindings
| Key | Command | Description |
|-----|---------|-------------|
| `C-c c c` | claude-code | Start Claude Code session |
| `C-c c r` | Send region | Send selected code to Claude |
| `C-c c s` | Send command | Send a command to Claude |
| `C-c c t` | Toggle window | Show/hide Claude window |
| `C-c c k` | Kill instance | Kill Claude session |

### Markdown Preview
Live preview with side-by-side editing using EWW (built-in browser).

| Key | Command | Description |
|-----|---------|-------------|
| `C-c C-c l` | Live preview | Toggle live preview (updates as you type) |
| `C-c C-c p` | Preview | Open preview in external browser |
| `C-c C-c v` | Export & view | Export HTML and open in browser |
| `C-c C-c g` | grip-mode | GitHub-flavored preview (exact GitHub rendering) |

**Mermaid diagram support:** Install `npm install -g @mermaid-js/mermaid-cli mermaid-filter` to render Mermaid diagrams. Use `C-c C-c p` (external browser) for Mermaid since EWW has limited image support.

**GitHub preview:** Run `go install github.com/chrishrb/go-grip@latest` for GitHub-flavored rendering with `C-c C-c g` (works offline, no API limits).

## Installation

### 1. Clone Repository
```bash
git clone https://github.com/nsega/.emacs.d.git ~/.emacs.d
```

### 2. Install System Dependencies
```bash
# Required for Japanese search
brew install cmigemo

# Highly recommended for fast search
brew install ripgrep

# Optional: Markdown support
brew install pandoc

# Terminal support (required for Claude Code)
brew install libvterm

# Claude Code CLI (for AI pair programming)
brew install --cask claude-code
```

### 3. Install LSP Servers (for code intelligence)

#### Python
```bash
pip install python-lsp-server
# Or for better performance:
pip install basedpyright
```

#### Go
```bash
go install golang.org/x/tools/gopls@latest
```

#### TypeScript
```bash
npm install -g typescript-language-server typescript
```

#### Java
```bash
brew install jdtls
```

#### Kotlin
```bash
brew install kotlin-language-server
```

#### YAML (optional)
```bash
npm install -g yaml-language-server
```

### 4. Install Tree-sitter Grammars (optional but recommended)

Tree-sitter provides accurate syntax highlighting. Install grammars in Emacs:
```
M-x treesit-install-language-grammar RET c
M-x treesit-install-language-grammar RET cpp
M-x treesit-install-language-grammar RET python
M-x treesit-install-language-grammar RET go
M-x treesit-install-language-grammar RET javascript
M-x treesit-install-language-grammar RET typescript
M-x treesit-install-language-grammar RET java
M-x treesit-install-language-grammar RET kotlin
```

### 5. Launch Emacs
```bash
emacs
```

On first launch, all packages install automatically from MELPA. This may take 1-2 minutes.

## Essential Key Bindings

### Most Important (Learn These First!)
| Key | Command | Description |
|-----|---------|-------------|
| `M-.` | Jump to definition | LSP-powered navigation |
| `M-,` | Go back | Like browser back button |
| `M-?` | Find references | Show all uses of symbol |
| `M-g i` | Jump in file | Jump to function/section (imenu) |
| `M-s l` | Search buffer | Search with live preview |
| `C-x p f` | Find file | Find file in project |
| `C-x b` | Switch buffer | Buffer list with preview |

### Completion (Vertico)
| Key | Command | Description |
|-----|---------|-------------|
| `M-x` | Execute command | Command palette with vertico |
| `C-x b` | consult-buffer | Switch buffers (shows recent files) |
| `C-x C-f` | find-file | Find/open file |
| `C-j / C-n` | Next item | In vertico completion |
| `C-k / C-p` | Previous item | In vertico completion |

### Search & Navigation
| Key | Command | Description |
|-----|---------|-------------|
| `M-s l` | consult-line | Search lines in buffer (live!) |
| `M-s r` | consult-ripgrep | Fast project-wide search |
| `M-s g` | consult-grep | Grep search in directory |
| `M-g i` | consult-imenu | Jump to function/section |

### Project Management (project.el)
| Key | Command | Description |
|-----|---------|-------------|
| `C-x p p` | Switch project | List known projects |
| `C-x p f` | Find file | Find file in project |
| `C-x p g` | Find regexp | Search across project |
| `C-x p b` | Switch buffer | Project buffers only |
| `C-x p k` | Kill buffers | Kill all project buffers |
| `C-x p e` | Eshell | Open eshell in project root |

### Code Intelligence (LSP via Eglot)
| Key | Command | Description |
|-----|---------|-------------|
| `M-.` | xref-find-definitions | Jump to definition |
| `M-?` | xref-find-references | Find all references |
| `M-,` | xref-pop-marker-stack | Go back |
| `C-h .` | eldoc-doc-buffer | Show documentation |
| `C-c r` | eglot-rename | Rename symbol everywhere |

### Editing
| Key | Command | Description |
|-----|---------|-------------|
| `C-;` | iedit-mode | Edit all occurrences |
| `M-%` | anzu-query-replace | Replace with count |
| `C-M-%` | anzu-query-replace-regexp | Regex replace |
| `M-w` | Copy line | Copy line (no selection needed) |
| `C-w` | Cut line | Cut line (no selection needed) |
| `C-c i` | Indent buffer/region | Auto-indent |
| `C-a` | Smart beginning of line | Toggle indent/start |
| `M-o` | Smart open line | Insert line below/above |

### Advanced
| Key | Command | Description |
|-----|---------|-------------|
| `C-.` | embark-act | Show actions for thing at point |
| `C-h B` | embark-bindings | Show all key bindings |

For complete reference, see **[USER_GUIDE.md](USER_GUIDE.md)**

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# .emacs.d

Modern Emacs configuration optimized for Emacs 30+ using best practices and contemporary tools.

## Overview

This configuration has been fully modernized (January 2026) to use:
- **use-package** for declarative package management
- **Vertico ecosystem** for lightweight, fast completion
- **Eglot** for LSP-powered code intelligence
- **Tree-sitter** for accurate syntax highlighting (with fallbacks)
- **early-init.el** for zero-flash startup
- **Pixel-precise scrolling** for native macOS trackpad feel
- **no-littering** for clean repository structure
- **Modern advice-add** (no deprecated code)
- **Single init.el** (consolidated from 6 files)

## Quick Start

```bash
# Clone the repository
git clone https://github.com/nsega/.emacs.d.git ~/.emacs.d

# Install dependencies (see below)
brew install cmigemo ripgrep

# Launch Emacs (packages install automatically)
emacs
```

For detailed usage, see the **[Learning Resources](#learning-resources)** below.

## Requirements

- **Emacs 30.x** or later (29.x also works)
- **macOS** (tested on Apple Silicon)
- **Homebrew** for package management

### Optional but Recommended
```bash
brew install ripgrep      # Fast project search
brew install pandoc       # Markdown preview
```

## Key Features

### Completion Framework (Vertico Ecosystem)
- **vertico** - Fast vertical completion UI
- **consult** - Enhanced commands (buffer switching, search, etc.)
- **marginalia** - Rich annotations in minibuffer
- **embark** - Contextual actions on completion candidates
- **orderless** - Flexible matching (type words in any order!)

### Code Intelligence (Eglot LSP)
- Jump to definition / Find references
- Code completion with Company
- Real-time diagnostics
- Symbol renaming across files
- Documentation on hover

### Project Management
- **project.el** (built-in) - Native project-aware navigation
- Auto-detects project roots (git, go.mod, etc.)
- Fast file finding and project-wide search
- Standard `C-x p` prefix (Emacs default)

### Editing Enhancements
- **yasnippet** - Template system
- **smartparens** - Smart parenthesis handling
- **iedit** - Multi-cursor editing
- **undo-tree** - Visual undo history
- Smart line copy/cut (no selection needed)
- Auto-indent on yank

### macOS Optimizations
- **Pixel-scroll-precision-mode** - Smooth trackpad scrolling (feels native)
- **early-init.el** - Zero-flash startup (UI disabled before frame init)
- **exec-path-from-shell** - Proper PATH inheritance from shell
- **no-littering** - Clean .emacs.d (files organized in var/ and etc/)

### Language Support
| Language   | Mode               | LSP Server                     | Install Command |
|------------|--------------------|--------------------------------|-----------------|
| Python     | python-ts-mode     | pylsp / basedpyright           | `pip install python-lsp-server` |
| Go         | go-ts-mode         | gopls                          | `go install golang.org/x/tools/gopls@latest` |
| JavaScript | js-ts-mode         | typescript-language-server     | `npm install -g typescript-language-server typescript` |
| TypeScript | typescript-ts-mode | typescript-language-server     | `npm install -g typescript-language-server typescript` |
| Java       | java-ts-mode       | jdtls                          | `brew install jdtls` |
| Kotlin     | kotlin-mode        | kotlin-language-server         | `brew install kotlin-language-server` |
| Groovy     | groovy-mode        | -                              | (syntax highlighting only) |
| C/C++      | c-ts-mode / c++-ts-mode | clangd                    | `xcode-select --install` or `brew install llvm` |
| YAML       | yaml-ts-mode       | yaml-language-server (opt)     | `npm install -g yaml-language-server` |

**Auto-formatting:** YAML files are automatically formatted on save using [apheleia](https://github.com/radian-software/apheleia) + [yamlfmt](https://github.com/google/yamlfmt). Install: `go install github.com/google/yamlfmt/cmd/yamlfmt@latest`
| Markdown   | markdown-mode      | -                              | `brew install pandoc` (for preview) |

**Tree-sitter modes** (`*-ts-mode`) provide accurate syntax highlighting. Grammars install via `M-x treesit-install-language-grammar`.

### Terminal Integration
- **vterm** - Full-featured terminal emulator
- **eat** - Pure elisp terminal (fallback)
- **Claude Code** - AI pair programming with Claude CLI

#### Claude Code Key Bindings
| Key | Command | Description |
|-----|---------|-------------|
| `C-c c c` | claude-code | Start Claude Code session |
| `C-c c r` | Send region | Send selected code to Claude |
| `C-c c s` | Send command | Send a command to Claude |
| `C-c c t` | Toggle window | Show/hide Claude window |
| `C-c c k` | Kill instance | Kill Claude session |

### Markdown Preview
Live preview with side-by-side editing using EWW (built-in browser).

| Key | Command | Description |
|-----|---------|-------------|
| `C-c C-c l` | Live preview | Toggle live preview (updates as you type) |
| `C-c C-c p` | Preview | Open preview in external browser |
| `C-c C-c v` | Export & view | Export HTML and open in browser |
| `C-c C-c g` | grip-mode | GitHub-flavored preview (exact GitHub rendering) |

**Mermaid diagram support:** Install `npm install -g @mermaid-js/mermaid-cli mermaid-filter` to render Mermaid diagrams. Use `C-c C-c p` (external browser) for Mermaid since EWW has limited image support.

**GitHub preview:** Run `go install github.com/chrishrb/go-grip@latest` for GitHub-flavored rendering with `C-c C-c g` (works offline, no API limits).

## Installation

### 1. Clone Repository
```bash
git clone https://github.com/nsega/.emacs.d.git ~/.emacs.d
```

### 2. Install System Dependencies
```bash
# Required for Japanese search
brew install cmigemo

# Highly recommended for fast search
brew install ripgrep

# Optional: Markdown support
brew install pandoc

# YAML auto-formatting
go install github.com/google/yamlfmt/cmd/yamlfmt@latest

# Terminal support (required for Claude Code)
brew install libvterm

# Claude Code CLI (for AI pair programming)
brew install --cask claude-code
```

### 3. Install LSP Servers (for code intelligence)

#### Python
```bash
pip install python-lsp-server
# Or for better performance:
pip install basedpyright
```

#### Go
```bash
go install golang.org/x/tools/gopls@latest
```

#### TypeScript
```bash
npm install -g typescript-language-server typescript
```

#### Java
```bash
brew install jdtls
```

#### Kotlin
```bash
brew install kotlin-language-server
```

#### YAML (optional)
```bash
npm install -g yaml-language-server
```

### 4. Install Tree-sitter Grammars (optional but recommended)

Tree-sitter provides accurate syntax highlighting. Install grammars in Emacs:
```
M-x treesit-install-language-grammar RET c
M-x treesit-install-language-grammar RET cpp
M-x treesit-install-language-grammar RET python
M-x treesit-install-language-grammar RET go
M-x treesit-install-language-grammar RET javascript
M-x treesit-install-language-grammar RET typescript
M-x treesit-install-language-grammar RET java
M-x treesit-install-language-grammar RET kotlin
```

### 5. Launch Emacs
```bash
emacs
```

On first launch, all packages install automatically from MELPA. This may take 1-2 minutes.

## Essential Key Bindings

### Most Important (Learn These First!)
| Key | Command | Description |
|-----|---------|-------------|
| `M-.` | Jump to definition | LSP-powered navigation |
| `M-,` | Go back | Like browser back button |
| `M-?` | Find references | Show all uses of symbol |
| `M-g i` | Jump in file | Jump to function/section (imenu) |
| `M-s l` | Search buffer | Search with live preview |
| `C-x p f` | Find file | Find file in project |
| `C-x b` | Switch buffer | Buffer list with preview |

### Completion (Vertico)
| Key | Command | Description |
|-----|---------|-------------|
| `M-x` | Execute command | Command palette with vertico |
| `C-x b` | consult-buffer | Switch buffers (shows recent files) |
| `C-x C-f` | find-file | Find/open file |
| `C-j / C-n` | Next item | In vertico completion |
| `C-k / C-p` | Previous item | In vertico completion |

### Search & Navigation
| Key | Command | Description |
|-----|---------|-------------|
| `M-s l` | consult-line | Search lines in buffer (live!) |
| `M-s r` | consult-ripgrep | Fast project-wide search |
| `M-s g` | consult-grep | Grep search in directory |
| `M-g i` | consult-imenu | Jump to function/section |

### Project Management (project.el)
| Key | Command | Description |
|-----|---------|-------------|
| `C-x p p` | Switch project | List known projects |
| `C-x p f` | Find file | Find file in project |
| `C-x p g` | Find regexp | Search across project |
| `C-x p b` | Switch buffer | Project buffers only |
| `C-x p k` | Kill buffers | Kill all project buffers |
| `C-x p e` | Eshell | Open eshell in project root |

### Code Intelligence (LSP via Eglot)
| Key | Command | Description |
|-----|---------|-------------|
| `M-.` | xref-find-definitions | Jump to definition |
| `M-?` | xref-find-references | Find all references |
| `M-,` | xref-pop-marker-stack | Go back |
| `C-h .` | eldoc-doc-buffer | Show documentation |
| `C-c r` | eglot-rename | Rename symbol everywhere |

### Editing
| Key | Command | Description |
|-----|---------|-------------|
| `C-;` | iedit-mode | Edit all occurrences |
| `M-%` | anzu-query-replace | Replace with count |
| `C-M-%` | anzu-query-replace-regexp | Regex replace |
| `M-w` | Copy line | Copy line (no selection needed) |
| `C-w` | Cut line | Cut line (no selection needed) |
| `C-c i` | Indent buffer/region | Auto-indent |
| `C-a` | Smart beginning of line | Toggle indent/start |
| `M-o` | Smart open line | Insert line below/above |

### Advanced
| Key | Command | Description |
|-----|---------|-------------|
| `C-.` | embark-act | Show actions for thing at point |
| `C-h B` | embark-bindings | Show all key bindings |

For complete reference, see **[USER_GUIDE.md](USER_GUIDE.md)**

## Learning Resources

This repository includes comprehensive documentation:

### 1. **[USER_GUIDE.md](USER_GUIDE.md)** - Complete Reference
The comprehensive guide covering everything you need:
- Quick reference: Essential key bindings organized by category
- Workflows: 6 common patterns for reading code
- Terminal tips: Terminal-specific compatibility and configuration
- Advanced features: Power user techniques
- Troubleshooting: Solutions to common problems

### 2. **[TUTORIAL.md](TUTORIAL.md)** - Hands-On Exercises
Step-by-step interactive tutorial:
- 6 exercises using real demo code (Go projects)
- Obsidian vault navigation examples
- Power user workflows
- Practice challenges
- Real-world tasks

**Recommended learning path:**
1. Read USER_GUIDE.md Quick Reference section
2. Try exercises 1-3 in TUTORIAL.md
3. Reference USER_GUIDE.md workflows as needed
4. **Terminal users:** See USER_GUIDE.md Terminal Emacs section

## Directory Structure

```
~/.emacs.d/
├── init.el                      # Main configuration (single file!)
├── early-init.el                # Early initialization (UI, GC optimization)
├── README.md                    # This file
├── USER_GUIDE.md               # Complete user guide (reference + workflows + terminal tips)
├── TUTORIAL.md                 # Hands-on exercises and tutorials
├── CLAUDE.md                   # Guidelines for Claude Code
├── LICENSE                     # MIT License
├── .claude/
│   ├── plans/
│   │   └── modernization-for-emacs30.md  # Modernization plan
│   └── settings.local.json     # Local Claude settings
├── elpa/                       # Installed packages (auto-generated)
├── snippets/                   # Yasnippet templates
├── tree-sitter/                # Tree-sitter grammars (auto-generated)
└── custom.backup.*/            # Backups from modernization
```

**Note:** The old `custom/` directory structure has been consolidated into a single `init.el` using use-package.

## Usage Examples

### Opening a Project
```bash
cd /path/to/your/project
emacs .
```

Emacs opens in Dired (directory editor):
- Press `RET` on files to open them
- Press `^` to go up to parent directory
- Or use `C-x p f` to find files quickly

### Exploring Code
```
1. C-x p f     → Find file in project
2. M-g i       → See all functions
3. M-.         → Jump to definition
4. M-?         → Find references
5. M-,         → Go back
```

### Searching Across Project
```
M-s r          → Type search term
→ Navigate results with C-n/C-p
→ Press RET to jump to match
```

### Renaming a Symbol
```
M-.            → Jump to definition
C-c r          → eglot-rename
→ Type new name
→ RET          → Renames everywhere!
```

## Customization

### Adding New Packages

All packages use `use-package`. Add to `init.el`:

```elisp
(use-package your-package
  :config
  ;; Your configuration
  :bind (("C-c y" . your-command)))
```

### Configuring LSP for New Languages

```elisp
(use-package your-mode
  :hook (your-mode . eglot-ensure)
  :config
  ;; Optional: custom LSP server
  (add-to-list 'eglot-server-programs
               '(your-mode . ("your-lsp-server" "--args"))))
```

### Performance Tuning

Check package load times:
```
M-x use-package-report
```

## Troubleshooting

### LSP Not Working?

1. **Check if Eglot is active:** Look for "Eglot" in mode line
2. **Manually start:** `M-x eglot-ensure`
3. **Restart server:** `M-x eglot-reconnect`
4. **Check server is installed:**
   ```bash
   which gopls
   which pylsp
   which typescript-language-server
   ```

### Search is Slow?

Install ripgrep for blazing fast search:
```bash
brew install ripgrep
```
Then use `M-s r` instead of `M-s g`

### Packages Won't Install?

```bash
# Clean package cache
rm -rf ~/.emacs.d/elpa
emacs  # Packages reinstall automatically
```

### Can't Find Commands?

Check available commands:
```
M-x consult-  [TAB]    # See all consult commands
M-x project-  [TAB]    # See all project commands
C-h B                  # Show all key bindings
```

### Japanese Input Not Working?

```bash
brew install cmigemo
```

Verify dictionary path in init.el matches:
```bash
ls /opt/homebrew/share/migemo/utf-8/migemo-dict
```

### Emacs Opens in Home Directory Instead of Current Directory?

This was fixed in the modernization. Make sure you have the latest version:
```bash
cd ~/.emacs.d
git pull
```

## What's Different from Traditional Configs?

### Before Modernization
- 6 separate files (init.el + custom/*.el)
- Helm for completion (heavy)
- Multiple navigation systems (helm-gtags, ggtags, CEDET)
- Deprecated `defadvice` code
- Manual package management

### After Modernization
- **Single init.el** with use-package
- **Vertico ecosystem** (20-30% faster)
- **Single LSP system** (Eglot only)
- **Tree-sitter parsing** (accurate syntax highlighting)
- **early-init.el** (zero-flash startup)
- **Pixel-precise scrolling** (native macOS feel)
- **no-littering** (clean directory structure)
- **Modern advice-add** (no deprecated code)
- **Declarative package management**

**Result:** Simpler, faster, more maintainable, and feels like a native macOS app!

## Advanced Features

### Orderless Matching

Type search terms in any order:
- `"user test"` matches `test_user_function` or `user_management_test`
- Works in all vertico completions

### Live Previews

- `C-x b` - Preview buffers before switching
- `M-s l` - See matches as you type
- `C-c C-c l` - Markdown live preview (in markdown files)
- Navigate with `C-n`/`C-p` to peek

### Multi-Edit (iedit)

```
M-s l          → Find pattern
C-;            → Edit all occurrences at once
→ Type changes
C-;            → Exit iedit
```

### Embark Actions

```
C-x b          → Buffer list
→ Select buffer
C-.            → Show actions (view, save, kill, diff, etc.)
```

## Performance

- **Startup time:** ~2-3 seconds (packages lazy-load)
- **Search:** < 0.1s for most projects (with ripgrep)
- **LSP response:** Real-time (< 100ms)
- **Memory:** ~50-100MB base, ~200MB with multiple LSP servers

Use `M-x use-package-report` to analyze package load times.

## Contributing

This is a personal configuration, but improvements are welcome:

1. Fork the repository
2. Create a feature branch
3. Make your changes
4. Submit a pull request

## License

MIT License - See LICENSE file for details

## Acknowledgments

Built with these amazing packages:
- [vertico](https://github.com/minad/vertico) - Vertical completion
- [consult](https://github.com/minad/consult) - Consulting completing-read
- [marginalia](https://github.com/minad/marginalia) - Rich annotations
- [embark](https://github.com/oantolin/embark) - Contextual actions
- [company](https://github.com/company-mode/company-mode) - Completion
- [eglot](https://github.com/joaotavora/eglot) - LSP client
- [claude-code.el](https://github.com/stevemolitor/claude-code.el) - Claude Code integration
- [vterm](https://github.com/akermu/emacs-libvterm) - Terminal emulator

## Additional Resources

- [Emacs Wiki](https://www.emacswiki.org/)
- [Vertico Documentation](https://github.com/minad/vertico)
- [Eglot Manual](https://joaotavora.github.io/eglot/)
- [project.el Manual](https://www.gnu.org/software/emacs/manual/html_node/emacs/Projects.html)

---

**Last Updated:** January 2026
**Emacs Version:** 30.x
**Status:** Production Ready

For questions or issues, please open an issue on GitHub.
